name: Tarifa and Facinas Loop via Los Alcornocales
name_original: De Tarifa a Facinas – circular por el Parque Natural los Alcornocales
description: This moderate 28.6-mile road cycling route from Tarifa to Facinas explores the cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park.
region_intro: Road cycling around Tarifa offers varied terrain at the southernmost tip of continental Europe, where the Mediterranean Sea meets the Atlantic Ocean. The region features coastal paths with views of the Strait of Gibraltar, mountainous areas, and extensive cork oak forests within natural parks. Golden sand dunes, beaches, and hills rising directly from the sea characterize the landscape, providing diverse environments for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Tarifa The most popular road cycling route is Tarifa and Facinas Loop via Los Alcornocales, a 28.6 miles (46.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route traverses the dense cork oak forests of Los Alcornocales Natural Park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Bologna to Zahara de los Atunes via the Facinas Pass, a moderate 41.3 miles (66.5 km) path. This route offers coastal views and includes the ascent over the Facinas Pass. Local road cyclists also love the Facinas Entrance Sign – Facinas Village loop from Torre de la Peña, a 19.6 miles (31.5 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 47 minutes. Road cycling around Tarifa is defined by coastal paths, cork oak forests, and hills, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Tarifa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 1100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Tarifa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Tarifa?
A: There are over 20 dedicated road cycling routes around Tarifa, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I encounter on road cycling routes in Tarifa?
A: Road cycling in Tarifa offers a mix of stunning landscapes. You can expect coastal paths with breathtaking views of the Strait of Gibraltar and the African coast, routes through the dense cork oak forests of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957645362/tarifa-and-facinas-loop-via-los-alcornocales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Alcornocales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and mountainous areas that rise directly from the sea, providing challenging climbs and rewarding vistas.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Tarifa?
A: Yes, Tarifa offers several easier road cycling routes. While the region is known for its varied terrain, there are 3 routes specifically graded as easy, perfect for those new to road cycling or looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes often feature gentler gradients and less traffic.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Tarifa?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Tarifa has 2 routes graded as difficult. These routes typically feature significant elevation gains and longer distances, such as the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31538673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarinal Lighthouse – Los Caños de Meca loop from La Costa&lt;/a&gt;, which includes over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa generally offers a pleasant climate for road cycling year-round. However, the consistent breeze, famous for wind sports, can add a challenging dimension, especially strong Levante or Poniente winds. Spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures and more predictable wind conditions for cycling.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Tarifa?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Tarifa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957645362/tarifa-and-facinas-loop-via-los-alcornocales" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tarifa and Facinas Loop via Los Alcornocales&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through diverse landscapes and returns to your starting point.

Q: What historical landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Tarifa often pass by significant historical sites and offer spectacular viewpoints. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/255197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guzmán el Bueno Castle&lt;/a&gt; in Tarifa's old town, or ride past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de Tarifa – southernmost point of continental Europe&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/838636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; also offers panoramic views of the surrounding area.

Q: Are there natural attractions to explore while road cycling in Tarifa?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/370696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolonia Dune&lt;/a&gt;, a stunning natural monument, or experience the unique geography at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6194993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Meeting Point of the Atlantic Ocean and Mediterranean Sea&lt;/a&gt;. Routes also traverse the diverse landscapes of the Strait Natural Park and Los Alcornocales Natural Park.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Tarifa?
A: The road cycling routes in Tarifa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the stunning coastal views, and the opportunity to explore natural parks like Los Alcornocales, which offers a unique cycling experience through cork oak forests.

Q: Are there any routes that offer coastal views?
A: Many routes in Tarifa feature spectacular coastal views. For instance, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975543417/from-bologna-to-zahara-de-los-atunes-via-the-facinas-pass" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Bologna to Zahara de los Atunes via the Facinas Pass&lt;/a&gt; provides extensive coastal vistas as it winds along the Atlantic side, offering glimpses of the sea and the iconic kitesurfing beaches.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Tarifa?
A: Tarifa town center and surrounding villages often have public parking areas suitable for starting your ride. For routes venturing into natural parks or more remote areas, it's advisable to check specific starting points for available parking, which is generally accessible near trailheads or local amenities.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through rural villages?
A: Yes, some routes will take you through charming rural landscapes and villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21811912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Facinas Entrance Sign – Facinas Village loop from Torre de la Peña&lt;/a&gt; is a good example, leading you through the countryside and offering a glimpse into local life outside of Tarifa's main town.
tips: Castillo de Guzmán el Bueno was restored in the first half of the 17th century, when it still maintained its defensive activity as a bastion against the Barbary piracy that devastated these coasts during the 16th and 17th centuries.
This gate marks the main entrance to the old town of Tarifa.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the 'From Tarifa to Facinas' road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally easy to ride for road cyclists. Approximately 42.7 km of the route is asphalt and 3.30 km is paved, ensuring a smooth experience through the natural park.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'moderate road ride' and requires good fitness levels. While it's not as challenging as some 'hard' routes in the region, it is more demanding than 'easy' routes. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What natural features and wildlife can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through a quieter section of Los Alcornocales Natural Park, Europe's largest cork oak forest. Expect to see dense cork oak forests, open pastures, and sweeping hills. Depending on the season, you might observe bird migration in spring and fall, and potentially encounter wildlife such as deer or birds of prey.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks or viewpoints on this route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can pass by historical sites like Guzmán el Bueno Castle and Puerta de Jerez in Tarifa. You'll also traverse the scenic Puerto de Facinas and ride along the beautiful Ojén Road in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales, offering stunning lookouts and coastal views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route in Tarifa?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Tarifa, you can typically find parking within the town. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but general public parking lots or street parking in Tarifa would be your best option to begin the circular tour.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers beautiful scenery year-round. Spring and fall are particularly pleasant for cycling, with comfortable temperatures and the added bonus of potentially observing bird migration. Summers can be hot, so early morning rides are recommended during those months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in Los Alcornocales Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within Los Alcornocales Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to respect park regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife.

faqs.question: Is the 'From Tarifa to Facinas' route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebike cycling on paved roads. While dogs are generally allowed in Los Alcornocales Natural Park, bringing a dog on a racebike route of this nature is not typically recommended or practical. If you plan to bring a dog, consider alternative walking or hiking trails within the park that are more suitable for pets.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it covers approximately 55% of the Transandalus trail, 20% of the Sendero Europeo Arco Atlantico - Etapa 1, 14% of the Andalucía (Province of Cádiz) route, and 13% of the Camino de Santiago del Estrecho.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Facinas, where you can find local cafes or shops to refuel. Starting and ending in Tarifa also provides ample opportunities for refreshments and amenities before and after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a racebike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given it's a moderate racebike route, you should bring your racebike, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water bottles (enough for approximately 2 hours of riding), snacks, a basic repair kit, and sun protection. A phone with the komoot app for navigation is also highly recommended.

waypoints.name: Ojén Road in Parque Natural Los Alcornocales
